title: Orez <> rocicorp/mono upstream Zero conformance coverage ledger
purpose: Machine-readable map of upstream ZQL/Zero conformance assets (the coverage-driven fuzzer and the differential/protocol test suites) to their Orez black-box-harness status, so parity is auditable and drift is explicit. Owned by the upstream-parity worker; see README.md. Exhaustive per-file accounting is in inventory.json; both are verified by verify.ts.

statusVocabulary.upstream-baseline: Upstream asset is the reference/spec; Orez conforms to it rather than porting it (e.g. the v51 wire schemas, the coverage model).
statusVocabulary.ported-black-box: Orez has a wire-level / black-box equivalent that runs against a real server (no dependency on mono's in-process IVM).
statusVocabulary.equivalent: Orez fully re-expresses the same property by a different mechanism (e.g. cross-implementation wire differential instead of in-process IVM-vs-pg; late-client rehydrate instead of in-process push history).
statusVocabulary.equivalent-partial: Orez re-expresses the behavior black-box but with a NAMED missing piece (e.g. differential runs vs a reference impl but without the pairwise-coverage gate / bounded enumeration / shrink / scale-differential upstream also has). Behavioral coverage is real but not complete; the gap is stated.
statusVocabulary.portable-gap: Portable and valuable, but not yet built in Orez. Reason names the value and why it is safe to port.
statusVocabulary.not-applicable: Cannot or should not be ported black-box AND Orez has no black-box behavioral analog. Reason names the coupling (mono planner/plan-flag/in-process transform) or the app/demo scope. NOTE: a test being implementation-coupled (in-process) does NOT by itself make it not-applicable — if Orez re-expresses the behavior black-box it is equivalent/equivalent-partial. Test portability and behavioral coverage are tracked separately (see inventory.json).

meta.orezBaselineNote: The mono HEAD the Orez conformance harness was originally built against (plans/zero-conformance-harness.md). Retained ONLY as the historical baseline so drift from auditedUpstreamSha is explicit; do not re-pin work to it.
meta.zeroPin: @rocicorp/zero 1.7.0 (harness/package.json). Sync protocol v51. Client-side query evaluation (zqlite/IVM) is 1.7.0 on every snapshot-shipping target.
meta.driftCheck: bun harness/upstream-parity/verify.ts (fetches origin/main, verifies inventory counts + the exact 24-file set at the audited SHA, and flags drift auditedUpstreamSha..origin/main over conformance-relevant paths; exits non-zero on a stale audit or unacknowledged drift).

drift.note: 7 commits land between orezBaselineSha and auditedUpstreamSha. The fuzz generator directory (packages/zql-integration-tests/src/chinook/fuzz) is byte-identical across the range, so the ported-generator audit holds at the audited SHA. The drift is entirely query-semantics + zero-cache connection/DDL robustness.

commits.subject: perf(zbugs): hoist list Row out of ListPage (#6226)
fix(zqlite): compile NULL cursor bounds into null-safe start constraints (#6121)
commits.reason: Query-semantics fix NOT in the 1.7.0 pin. `col > NULL`/`col = NULL` matched nothing, so a start-cursor anchored in the NULL-sorted region silently returned empty. CONFIRMED 2026-07-11 (deterministic) on the stock zero-cache 1.7.0 reference by the new metamorphic startSuffix guard: start(dueAt asc, after {null,t1}, exclusive) returns [] vs a 46-row correct suffix (server-side sqlite table-source). orez-local PASSES the same shape (ships snapshots, materializes client-side, never pushes start into the buggy sqlite fetch). The stock-vs-orez differential misses it because the sweep generator has NO nullable-column start-cursor axis; had it emitted one, stock ([]) and orez (rows) would diverge and it would also flag it. So #6121 is a generator-coverage miss, not a both-sides-wrong shared blind spot. Repro: harness/regressions/known-gap-zql-6121-null-start-cursor.json.
upstreamTests: packages/zqlite/src/table-source.test.ts (+170: 'fetching across a NULL-sorted cursor region')
packages/zero-cache/src/db/lite-tables.test.ts (+51: optional column |NOT_NULL mirroring — the truthful `optional` flag the null-bound admission relies on)
commits.orezExistingCoverage: crates/sync-core/tests/query_ast.rs::start_cursor_null_ordering (GAP-2b) — Orez's Rust server-side compiler handles NULL cursors correctly and is tested. That is compiler-side/server-side; it does NOT cover the client-side 1.7.0 zqlite evaluator used by the snapshot-shipping harness targets.

commits.subject: chore: add MutatorResult to exported types (#6223)
feat(zero-cache): add optional newColumns field to DDL events (#6222)
commits.reason: Logical-replication DDL-evolution event. Relevant to Orez's pg-proxy/replication path, not to the snapshot-shipping sync-server core the harness exercises. Tests: change-source/pg/schema/ddl.pg.test.ts (+24), types/pg.test.ts (+127). Flagged for the replication planes, out of scope for this conformance harness.

commits.subject: fix(zero-cache): make the socket inactivity watchdog survive TLS upgrade (#6221)
commits.reason: zero-cache persistent-websocket robustness. Orez planes use the on-zero http-pull transport (no long-lived server socket), so this affects only the stock-zero reference stack, not an Orez conformance surface.

commits.subject: fix(zero-cache): reset half-open pg sockets after inactivity (#6220)
commits.reason: Upstream pg-connection robustness in zero-cache. Orez's pg handling is separate (pg-proxy / native pg / pglite); noted for those planes, not a black-box conformance surface.

fuzzModules.note: The 20-module coverage-driven fuzzer ported from rocicorp's internal rust `rindle-fuzz`. upstreamPath is relative to packages/zql-integration-tests/src/chinook/fuzz/. `portability` is intrinsic (can it leave mono's IVM?); `orezStatus` is where Orez stands.

modules.purpose: mulberry32 seeded PRNG for bit-exact replay
modules.note: Same PRNG family; deterministic-per-seed replay is preserved.

modules.purpose: schema read-model + per-table value roles + the 5 orthogonal decoration axes (filter x exists x order x limit x start)
modules.gap: No formal named AXES domains with realizability gates; the axis space is implicit in the generator. Blocks a covering-array / pairwise measurement (see coverage.ts, cover.ts).

modules.purpose: data-driven non-vacuous literals so filters actually partition the fixture
modules.orezArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts (TITLE_PATTERNS/NAME_PATTERNS + value generators) + SEED-derived id pools
modules.note: Literals tuned to SEED; sweep tracks a non-vacuous rate (returned-data-at-hydrate).

modules.purpose: t-way (pairwise) coverage accounting + feature-interaction tags + 100% missed-tuple gate
modules.gap: HIGH value. Orez prints raw axis counts but has no pairwise/t-way tuple accounting, no missed-tuple report, and no coverage gate. This is the measurement that turns 'we exercised shapes' into a number.

modules.purpose: static cost gate to skip pathologically expensive random-tail queries
modules.reason: Orez's generator bounds shape depth structurally (depth caps in genWhere/genSub); there is no unbounded random tail to gate. Low value at current scale.

modules.purpose: L0 bounded-exhaustive structural skeleton enumeration (smallest-first)
modules.gap: Orez does RANDOM skeletons, not bounded-exhaustive smallest-first enumeration, so there is no guarantee every small shape is covered. Moderate value; pairs with coverage accounting.

modules.purpose: L1 AETG greedy pairwise covering array + assignment lowering
modules.gap: No covering array; pure random generation. Pairs with coverage.ts to give a bounded, provably-pairwise-complete lane.

modules.purpose: L2 swarm testing — per-run random feature masks that disable axes
modules.note: Orez's per-axis probabilities are a weak swarm (each axis independently present/absent); no explicit whole-run feature-mask that disables axes. Partial.

modules.purpose: L3 mutation-from-corpus — one small twist on a known-good skeleton
modules.orezArtifact: none (harness/src/sweep.ts genWrites mutates DATA, not QUERIES)
modules.gap: Orez has no query-mutation-from-corpus generator (the neighborhood where minimal repros live). Lower value: sweep accumulates shapes across rounds, giving some incremental-maintenance neighborhood coverage.

modules.purpose: L4 pure-random deep-nesting long-tail generator, cost-gated
modules.orezArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts genSpec (recursive related depth<=2, and/or trees depth<=2)
modules.note: sweep's pure-random generator is the tail analog and the shape Orez most resembles. Depth is shallower than upstream's tail (<=2 vs <=4).

modules.purpose: four-phase net-zero push history (RemoveAll->AddBack->EditToRandom->EditToMatch) for incremental maintenance
modules.orezArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts genWrites (real wire mutations + upstream sql churn) + incremental==fresh late-client rehydrate
modules.note: Orez drives REAL v51 wire mutations and upstream sql across accumulating live views and checks incremental==fresh via a late client. Different mechanism, same invariant. MISSING: the explicit net-zero four-phase round-trip (RemoveAll->AddBack->EditToRandom->EditToMatch) that pins return-to-seed and catches transient strand/drop between mutations.

modules.purpose: flip-invariance — toggle the IVM plan flag (semi-join vs FlippedJoin) on positive EXISTS
modules.reason: `flip` is a mono IVM planner flag with no wire-side or pg analog; a black-box client cannot select query plans. Also `not(exists())` is unsupported on the zero client (bugs.rocicorp.dev/3438, found by the sweep lane).

modules.purpose: IVM-vs-IVM metamorphic relations (oracle-free): redundantConjunct, andReorder, largeLimit, startBeforeFirst
modules.note: Ports the 3 always-true invariant transforms (redundantConjunct/andReorder/largeLimit) AND adds computed startSuffix + limitPrefix, which upstream's metamorphic layer lacks (upstream only exercises NON-binding startBeforeFirst / largeLimit). Oracle-free: checks one target's self-consistency. EMPIRICALLY found #6121 on the stock 1.7.0 reference (startSuffix, null-anchored cursor -> stock returns [], orez-local correct); the differential missed it for lack of a nullable-start axis. Mutation-proven: harness/src/metamorphic.selftest.ts shows the guard goes red on a simulated #6121 empty-continuation and an off-by-one start; harness/src/metamorphic-lane.ts --mutate proves the live wiring catches it (24/24) without writing repros.

modules.purpose: structure-aware delta-debug shrinker -> minimal repro
modules.orezArtifact: none (harness/src/sweep.ts records the failing seed + FULL spec, unshrunk)
modules.gap: HIGH value. On a divergence Orez emits the whole random spec; there is no delta-debug to a minimal still-reproducing spec. Next asset after metamorphic; pairs with the committed replay corpus (regressions.ts).

modules.purpose: serialize/replay committed minimized repros first on every run
modules.orezArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts (writes divergence artifacts to harness/regressions/, which is intended tracked but is NOT loaded/replayed)
modules.gap: The artifact-writing half exists (and harness/.gitignore keeps regressions/ tracked), but the replay-FIRST gate — load committed minimized specs and re-run them before random rounds — is missing, so a past find is not a permanent guard. Pairs with shrink.ts.

modules.purpose: scalar-subquery lane mirroring zero-cache pipeline-driver pre-resolve
modules.reason: Hard runtime dependency on zqlite/src/resolve-scalar-subqueries.ts; the differential is only meaningful against the in-process IVM's non-native scalar handling. Not black-box portable.

modules.purpose: re-wrap a raw AST as a runnable mono Query
modules.orezArtifact: harness/src/fixture.ts buildGenerated (builds queries from GenSpec via the fluent builder; never leaves the builder, so no AST->Query rewrap is needed)

modules.purpose: deterministic ~40-row FK-consistent chinook fixture + DDL/seed generation
modules.note: Different schema by design (todo app modeling ~/chat's query layer vs chinook). Same single-source-of-truth discipline; fixture.ts fails loud if the TABLES mirror drifts from the zero schema.

modules.purpose: in-process orchestration: generate -> runAndCompare (IVM memory+sqlite vs pg oracle) -> capture -> minimize -> coverage
modules.note: sweep is Orez's driver: generate -> materialize on BOTH stock-zero and an orez target over the wire -> canonical compare -> record. The in-process IVM-vs-pg differential is replaced by a cross-IMPLEMENTATION wire differential (reference impl, not a fresh pg oracle), which is the property the rewrite must hold. MISSING vs upstream's driver: shrink.ts (minimize repro), coverage.ts (pairwise accounting/gate), and the scalar/flip axes.

modules.purpose: fast no-PG unit tests of the generator machinery (enumeration counts, covering-array completeness, metamorphic/flip self-consistency)
modules.orezArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts --dry (axis-count sanity) + harness/src/metamorphic.selftest.ts (metamorphic self-consistency + mutation proof)
modules.gap: MISSING enumeration-count / covering-array-completeness assertions (depends on the coverage.ts + skeleton.ts gaps).

conformanceSuites.note: Selected upstream test surfaces beyond the fuzzer, classified for a black-box harness. Paths relative to mono root.

suites.purpose: v51 wire-protocol valita schemas — the spec a wire client must conform to
suites.note: Orez conforms via the on-zero http-pull transport + src/sync-server; these schemas ARE the contract, not something to port. PROTOCOL_VERSION=51.

suites.purpose: L1 replica + L2 protocol-materialized differential vs a fresh pg oracle (richest oracle in the repo)
suites.portability: in-process (protocol-shaped but connects to view-syncer in-process; bypasses the socket)
suites.note: Orez's differential runs OVER THE WIRE (real stock @rocicorp/zero clients on the http-pull transport) where upstream's ProtocolFuzzerClient calls viewSyncer.initConnection directly. Orez trades the pg oracle for a stock-zero reference implementation.

suites.purpose: the only real over-the-wire websocket v51 test (asserts poke/protocol behavior; no pg-oracle diff)
suites.note: Orez drives real stock clients over the wire against every target; adds convergence + oracle equality upstream's integration test does not.

suites.purpose: query-result differential edges: bigint (int8), collation (byte-order vs locale), text-semantics (LIKE/ILIKE Unicode folding under COLLATE C), json/timestamps
suites.orezArtifact: harness/src/shapes.ts + harness/src/sweep.ts (cross-impl differential, but no dedicated edge fixtures)
suites.gap: Orez's differential compares cross-implementation but the fixture has no COLLATE-C table, no int8/bigint column, and does not pin the ILIKE-Unicode-folding known divergence. Value gap: these are exactly the per-target value-conversion edges the rowsPatch path must hold.

suites.note: Orez covers pull visibility + membership-driven revocation over the wire. Push-role authorization, auth-token refresh/staleness, and external-ACL invalidation remain greenfield — matching upstream, which punts permissions to the L4-future layer.

suites.purpose: pins known feature gaps: limit/orderBy not supported inside junction relationships
suites.note: Encode as harness xfails; the Orez generator must not generate junction-edge limit/orderBy (see knownXfails).

suites.reason: Orez ships snapshots (client-side evaluation) or compiles AST->SQL in the Rust core; it has no cost-based IVM planner, so the plan-choice tests have no analog.

invariants.note: The differential/metamorphic properties the upstream fuzzer checks, mapped to Orez lanes.

hydrateParity.orez: stock-zero result == orez-target result at hydrate (harness/src/shapes.ts, sweep.ts). Cross-implementation instead of vs-pg.

incrementalEqualsFresh.upstream: incrementally maintained view == fresh materialize after the same writes (driver.ts pushWalk, per step)
incrementalEqualsFresh.orez: accumulating live views stay canonical-equal under mirrored write churn, then late clients rehydrate every spec from scratch and must match the maintained view (sweep.ts).

metamorphicSelfConsistency.upstream: semantically-invariant AST transforms do not change the result, checked IVM-vs-IVM with NO oracle (metamorphic.ts)
metamorphicSelfConsistency.orez: harness/src/metamorphic.ts — oracle-free single-target self-consistency. Confirmed #6121 on the stock 1.7.0 reference (the differential missed it for lack of a nullable-start axis); also covers the class a differential is blind to by construction (both targets sharing a wrong behavior).

flipInvariance.orez: n/a — plan flag with no wire analog.

regressionCorpusSchema.note: Upstream's committed replay-artifact shape (regressions.ts). Shared with the consistency-checker arch worker so operation-history / repro artifacts stay compatible. Sources are NOT stored: the fixture is regenerated deterministically, so a case is just the minimized query (+ optional push history) as plain wire JSON.

upstreamShape.note: string — human description of the bug this guards, plus the original seed/label
upstreamShape.ast: AST — the shrunk query in zero-protocol wire JSON form
upstreamShape.pushes: Mutation[]? — optional four-phase push history; absent => a hydrate-only repro

regressionCorpusSchema.upstreamMutationShape: { table: string, kind: 'remove'|'add'|'edit', row: Row, old?: Row } (client table/column names)

orezAdaptation.note: Orez cases carry a GenSpec instead of a raw AST (the harness generates queries from GenSpec, never a bare AST), plus the seed/round for exact replay. The relate/mutation history is expressed as the harness Write[] script.
orezAdaptation.currentArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts recordDivergence() already writes this shape to harness/regressions/; the missing pieces are shrink (minimize spec) and a replay-first gate.

coverageModel.note: Upstream's coverage accounting (coverage.ts/cover.ts/axes.ts). Recorded so the Orez pairwise-coverage gap can be closed compatibly.
coverageModel.realizabilityGate: per-table only: a text filter needs a text column; an EXISTS needs an outgoing relationship. axes are otherwise orthogonal (no inter-axis constraint solver).
coverageModel.orezGap: Orez has no named axis domains and no t-way accounting; sweep --dry reports only raw per-axis presence counts. Note: upstream's start axis already includes null_exclusive, an axis Orez's generator does NOT cover (its start cursors are only on the non-null `rank` column). Extending the Orez generator to order-by a nullable column with a start cursor is what makes the metamorphic startSuffix guard exercise the #6121 region.

rankedGaps.value: Oracle-free single-target self-consistency: runs against any one target (incl. CF) with no reference impl. CONFIRMED #6121 on the stock 1.7.0 reference that the differential missed for lack of a nullable-start axis; also covers the harder class a differential is blind to by construction (both targets sharing a wrong behavior).
Turns each divergence into a minimal, permanent regression guard instead of a full random spec + gitignored artifact.
Turns 'we exercised shapes' into a gated number; surfaces uncovered axis-pairs.

rankedGaps.gap: value-conversion edge fixtures (collation / text-semantics / bigint / json)
rankedGaps.value: Pins the per-target rowsPatch value-conversion edges the fixture currently omits.
Guarantees every small query shape is covered, not just sampled.

knownXfails.reason: limit and orderBy are not supported inside junction relationships upstream (zql NotImplementedError; chinook.pg.test.ts brokenRelationshipLimits). The Orez generator must not emit them; the current fixture has no junction relationship so this is latent.
not(exists()) is unsupported on the zero client (bugs.rocicorp.dev/3438, found by the sweep lane). Orez uses junction-scoped whereExists instead.

#6121 (null-safe start constraints) lands 2026-07-10, after the 1.7.0 pin. CONFIRMED (deterministic, 2026-07-11) on the stock zero-cache 1.7.0 reference: the server-side sqlite table-source returns empty for a null-anchored exclusive start cursor. orez-local PASSES the same shape (client-side snapshot materialize). The metamorphic guard records it as a NON-GATING classified known-gap (harness/regressions/known-gap-zql-6121-null-start-cursor.json) that flips green once the harness pin advances past the fix.
